narrative: By the end, a technical reviewer should understand what NodeKit now proves, which real product slices support the claim, and which release gates remain open.
Use one thin NodeKit manifest, filesystem-discovered capabilities, composed evaluations, and content-addressed proof receipts.
Verified product slices remain distinct from planned deployment claims.
NodeKit v0.2.0 is source-released on GitHub; npm publication is blocked by registry authentication.
Deploy and production-prove the read-only synthetic Founder Quest Graph, then export and publish an evidence-bound deck, video, and campaign.

slides.body: One manifest connects runtime, evaluation, browser QA, and proof.
slides.headline: Empty directory. Proof-carrying application.
slides.title: What is NodeKit?

slides.body: Parity breaks when benchmark, browser, deployment, and public claims follow different implementations.
bullets: One implementation must serve every gate
slides.headline: The hard part is keeping every proof gate on one execution path.
slides.title: What pain is this solving?

slides.body: From empty directories, NodeKit installed, compiled, ran, evaluated, benchmarked, and proof-checked both presets.
slides.headline: Two presets passed the full factory path in 37.221 seconds.
slides.title: Does the empty-directory path actually work?

slides.body: On commit 6c8d7d9, the synthetic lab passed deterministic, live-provider, browser, reload, export, and identity gates.

formula.description: Synthetic local workflow only; no automated credit decision.

slides.headline: The Casca lab passed every local gate without making a credit decision.
slides.title: Can the system survive a real agentic product journey?

slides.body: The current baseline is behavior cloning, not RL. It passed protected local evaluation; the strict Azure-only live route failed closed with no fallback.
slides.headline: Protect the scorer first; train only after its boundaries are credible.
slides.title: How does NodeKit approach Agentic RL responsibly?

slides.body: After an exact hosted revision and fresh-user journey pass, the planned read-only demo will connect quests, sources, and proof.
slides.headline: Founder Quest remains planned until hosted and fresh-user proof pass.

image.altText: Founder Quest product view, draft placeholder
image.caption: Draft placeholder; hosted screenshot not supplied.

slides.metricLabel: hosted revision and fresh-user proof still required
slides.title: What does the graph make possible for a user?

slides.body: Anthropic's rare-disease grant is an independent forcing function for mapping research, novelty, evaluation, authority, and readiness, not an affiliation.
bullets: Research synthesis does not prove biology

formula.description: Research synthesis informs an application; it does not prove biology.

slides.headline: A real grant scenario tests the graph without claiming biological proof.
slides.title: How would a researcher use this today?

slides.body: Research, build, evaluation, proof, presentation, and feedback can watch one another; only external sources, commits, artifacts, and outcomes close the graph.
bullets: Inspect pass, fail, and human-review status

formula.description: External sources, commits, artifacts, and observed outcomes close the loop.

slides.headline: Loops need external anchors. Bring one workflow and inspect the proof.
slides.title: How do the pieces improve, and what should reviewers do next?

title: DRAFT — NodeKit launches NodeKit through proof-carrying product work
